Lift both legs a few inches off the ground, keeping them straight.
Alternate raising one leg while lowering the other in a scissor motion.
Continue to alternate legs in a controlled manner.
Keep your head and shoulders relaxed, core engaged.
breathing: Exhale as legs rise, inhale as legs lower.

name: Lying Straight Leg Raise
shortDescription: An exercise to isolate the hip flexors and lower abdominals from a supine position.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended and arms at your sides. Keeping legs straight and together, lift them to about 90 degrees, then lower under control without letting heels touch the ground.
steps: Lie flat on your back, arms at sides.
Extend legs fully, feet together.
Lift legs straight up to vertical.
Pause briefly at the top.
Lower legs down slowly but don’t touch floor.
breathing: Exhale as you lift legs, inhale as you lower.

name: Medicine Ball Wall Sit-Up
shortDescription: A dynamic core exercise involving a sit-up while throwing and catching a medicine ball against a wall.
instructions: Sit facing a wall with your knees bent, holding a medicine ball at your chest. Perform a sit-up and, at the top, throw the medicine ball against the wall, catch it as it rebounds, then lower back down and repeat.
steps: Sit on the floor facing a wall, knees bent.
Hold a medicine ball at your chest.
Lie back, then perform a sit-up.
At the top, throw the ball against the wall.
Catch the rebound and lower back down.
breathing: Exhale as you sit up/throw, inhale as you lower down.

name: Middle Back Stretch
shortDescription: A static stretch to lengthen the muscles of the thoracic (middle) back region.
instructions: Sit or stand with arms extended in front. Clasp your hands and round your upper back, reaching forward to feel a gentle stretch along your middle back. Hold the position without bouncing.
steps: Sit or stand with feet hip-width apart.
Extend your arms straight in front and clasp your hands.
Round your upper back and push your hands forward.
Tuck your chin slightly toward your chest.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds and release.
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back instead of rounding the upper back
breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly throughout the stretch.

name: Military Press
shortDescription: A standing barbell press that builds overall shoulder strength and stability.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell at shoulder height with an overhand grip. Press the bar overhead by extending your arms fully, keeping your core tight and avoiding excessive lower back arch. Lower the bar to the starting position under control.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and barbell at shoulder height.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder-width.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Press the barbell overhead to full arm extension.
Lower the bar slowly back to shoulders.
breathing: Exhale as you press up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Neck Side Stretch
shortDescription: A gentle static stretch that targets the side muscles of the neck, improving mobility and reducing tension.
instructions: Sit or stand upright, keeping good posture. Gently tilt your head toward one shoulder until a stretch is felt along the opposite side of the neck, holding for a set duration before switching sides.
steps: Sit or stand with good posture.
Slowly tilt your head to one side, ear towards shoulder.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Return to center.
Repeat on the opposite side.
commonMistakes: Turning the head instead of tilting
breathing: Breathe normally throughout the stretch.

name: Old School Reverse Extensions
shortDescription: This bodyweight movement isolates the triceps by extending the elbows while lying face down.
instructions: Lie face down on the floor or bench with arms bent and hands under shoulders. Extend your arms straight back by pressing your palms into the ground or lifting them off the ground, fully straightening your elbows and contracting your triceps. Lower back down under control.
steps: Lie face down on the floor or bench with elbows bent and hands at your sides.
Position elbows by your torso.
Straighten your arms fully behind you, contracting your triceps.
Pause and squeeze at full extension.
Return to the start in a controlled manner.
breathing: Exhale as you extend your arms, inhale as you return.

name: Otis-Up
shortDescription: A sit-up variation holding a weight at arm’s length overhead to increase abdominal engagement.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, holding a weight plate or dumbbell above your chest with arms extended. Sit up while keeping the weight overhead at arm's length, then lower back down under control.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet anchored.
Hold a weight with both hands, arms fully extended above your chest.
Engage your core and sit up, keeping arms straight and weight overhead.
Reach an upright seated position.
Lower yourself back down under control with arms extended.
breathing: Exhale as you sit up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Overhead Chest Stretch
shortDescription: A static stretch that opens the chest by extending the arms overhead and pulling back.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart, interlace your fingers and extend your arms overhead. Gently pull your arms back and upward, feeling a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
steps: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart.
Interlace fingers and stretch arms straight overhead.
Gently pull arms back behind head.
Squeeze shoulder blades together.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
breathing: Breathe deeply and steadily throughout the stretch.

name: Pec Deck Fly
shortDescription: A machine-based isolation exercise focusing on squeezing the chest muscles through a fly motion.
instructions: Sit on the pec deck machine with forearms against the pads and elbows slightly bent. Bring the arms together by contracting the chest, then slowly return to the starting position while maintaining control.
steps: Adjust seat and sit with back against pad.
Place forearms on pads with elbows slightly bent.
Bring arms together in a wide arc.
Squeeze chest at the end of movement.
Return slowly to starting position.
breathing: Exhale as you bring arms together, inhale as you open.

name: Peroneals Stretch
shortDescription: A static stretch that targets the outside muscles of the lower leg.
instructions: Sit or stand and cross one leg over the other, gently turning the sole of the foot inwards to feel a stretch along the outside of the lower leg. Hold the stretch, then repeat on the other side.
steps: Sit with one leg crossed over the other.
Grasp the foot and gently pull it inward.
Turn the sole toward your midline.
Hold for 20-30 seconds.
Switch sides and repeat.
breathing: Breathe normally throughout the stretch.

name: Pilates Corkscrew
shortDescription: A Pilates core exercise where the legs draw a circular motion to challenge abdominal stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with arms at your sides and legs extended overhead. Lift your hips slightly off the mat, then circle your legs in a controlled motion, using your core to stabilize and reverse the direction after each circle.
steps: Lie on back, arms by side, legs straight up.
Engage core, lift hips off mat gently.
Circle legs together in a clockwise motion.
Lower hips as legs complete the circle.
Reverse the circle direction.
breathing: Exhale as you circle legs, inhale to prepare.

name: Pilates Hundred
shortDescription: A classic Pilates exercise involving rhythmic arm pumps and core engagement.
instructions: Lie on your back with legs extended or in tabletop position. Lift your head, neck, and shoulders off the mat, extend arms, and pulse them up and down in small, quick movements while holding the core contracted for 100 counts.
steps: Lie on back, legs in tabletop or extended.
Lift head, neck, shoulders off mat.
Extend arms at sides, hovering above floor.
Pump arms up and down vigorously.
Inhale for 5 pumps, exhale for 5 pumps; repeat to 100.
breathing: Inhale for 5 pumps, exhale for 5 pumps, continue cycle.

name: Plyometric Side Lunge Stretch
shortDescription: A dynamic stretch to warm up and mobilize the hips, inner thighs, and legs.
instructions: Begin standing with feet shoulder-width apart. Step to the side into a deep lunge, keeping the opposite leg straight, and quickly alternate sides in a rhythmic motion.
steps: Stand with feet wider than hip-width apart.
Shift your weight to one side, bending the knee and keeping the opposite leg straight.
Drop into a deep lunge while maintaining upright posture.
Push off the bent leg and return to starting position.
Repeat to the opposite side in a dynamic, alternating fashion.
breathing: Inhale as you prepare and exhale moving into each lunge.

name: Pull-Up (Wide Grip)
shortDescription: A pull-up variation performed with a wide, overhand grip to emphasize the upper lats and back.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with hands wider than shoulders using an overhand grip. Pull yourself up until your chin is over the bar, then lower slowly.
steps: Grip pull-up bar with hands wider than shoulders, palms overhand.
Hang with arms fully extended and core tight.
Pull chest up to bar, driving elbows down and out.
Pause at the top, squeeze back muscles.
Lower down slowly to full extension.
breathing: Exhale as you pull up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Pull-Up / Chin-Up
shortDescription: A bodyweight pulling exercise that develops lat and upper body strength using a hanging bar.
instructions: Grab a pull-up bar with either a pronated (overhand, pull-up) or supinated (underhand, chin-up) grip slightly wider or narrower than shoulder width as desired. Start from a dead hang, retract your shoulders, and pull your chin above the bar using your back and arms. Control your descent back to the bottom.
steps: Stand under a pull-up bar and grip it with your chosen grip (overhand for pull-up, underhand for chin-up).
Hang at full arm extension with shoulders engaged.
Brace your core and pull your chest up toward the bar by driving elbows down.
Clear the bar with your chin or chest as you squeeze your lats.
Lower yourself under control to the starting hang position.
breathing: Exhale as you pull up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Push-Up
shortDescription: A bodyweight exercise performed in a prone position, targeting the chest, shoulders, and triceps.
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending elbows until your chest nearly touches the floor, then push back to the starting position while keeping your core engaged.
steps: Begin in a plank with hands under shoulders.
Engage core and glutes.
Lower chest toward floor, elbows at 45 degrees.
Pause briefly when close to ground.
Press back up to plank position.
breathing: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you push up.

name: Rear Decline Bridge
shortDescription: A bodyweight floor bridge that lifts the hips to train the glutes and hamstrings while keeping the shoulders on the ground.
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent. Place your arms by your sides with your palms facing down. Engage your glutes and hamstrings, and lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders. Hold this position for a few seconds, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place your arms by your sides and press your palms lightly into the floor.
Brace your midsection and squeeze your glutes.
Drive through your heels and lift your hips until your knees, hips, and shoulders line up.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping your ribs down.
Lower your hips to the floor with control and reset before the next rep.
commonMistakes: Pushing through the toes so the heels lift
Overarching the lower back at the top
Dropping the hips quickly to the floor
breathing: Inhale at the bottom, exhale as you lift your hips, and inhale again as you lower with control.

name: Rear Deltoid Stretch
shortDescription: A stretch that targets the rear shoulder to enhance flexibility and reduce tension.
instructions: Stand or sit upright, extend one arm across your body at shoulder height and use the opposite hand to pull it closer, feeling a stretch across the back of the shoulder. Hold, then switch sides.
steps: Extend one arm across your chest at shoulder height.
Use opposite hand to pull arm towards your body.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release and return to start.
Repeat on the other side.
breathing: Breathe normally throughout the stretch.

name: Rear Foot Elevated Hip Flexor Stretch
shortDescription: A deep hip flexor stretch with the rear foot elevated for improved flexibility.
instructions: Stand facing away from a bench or sturdy platform. Place one foot on the bench behind you and lower into a lunge, feeling a stretch in the hip flexors of the elevated leg. Keep your torso upright and pelvis tucked for maximum benefit.
steps: Stand with your back to a bench; place one foot on the bench.
Step the front foot forward and lower into a lunge.
Tuck pelvis under, keeping chest tall.
Lean gently forward to intensify the stretch.
Hold, then switch legs.
breathing: Inhale to prepare, exhale as you sink into the stretch.

name: Resistance Band High Fly
shortDescription: A chest isolation movement where resistance bands are used to fly arms downward from above shoulder height.
instructions: Attach bands above head height and hold one handle in each hand. Stand with arms overhead in a wide position, then pull hands down and together in an arc, squeezing the chest. Return slowly to the start.
steps: Anchor resistance band above shoulder height.
Grasp handles and step forward for tension.
Start with arms raised wide, aligned with shoulders.
Pull arms downward and together in a wide arc.
Squeeze chest, then return slowly to start.
breathing: Exhale as you bring arms together, inhale as you return.

name: Reverse Crunch
shortDescription: A bodyweight abs exercise lifting the hips toward the chest to target the lower abdominals.
instructions: Lie on your back with your arms by your sides and knees bent at 90 degrees. Engage your core and lift your hips off the floor, bringing your knees toward your chest, then slowly lower back to the starting position without letting your feet touch the floor.
steps: Lie on your back, arms at your sides, knees bent, and feet off the ground.
Engage your core and lift your legs, bringing knees toward your chest.
Lift hips off the floor in a curling motion.
Pause and squeeze your abs at the top.
Lower your hips back down slowly, keeping control.
commonMistakes: Letting lower back arch off the floor
breathing: Exhale as you lift hips, inhale as you lower down.

name: Reverse Dip Stretch
shortDescription: A stretch targeting the triceps and shoulders by extending the arms behind the body and lowering the torso.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and place your hands behind you with fingers facing forward. Gently press through your palms to lift your chest and feel the stretch across your upper arms and shoulders, holding the position without hyperextending your elbows.
steps: Sit with legs extended and arms behind your hips.
Place hands flat on the floor, fingers pointing forward.
Press through your palms to lift your chest up.
Keep your arms long without locking elbows.
Hold for the stretch, then slowly lower and relax.
breathing: Breathe deeply and steadily throughout the stretch.

name: Reverse Grip Machine Lat Pulldown
shortDescription: A machine pulldown using an underhand grip to train the lats through a vertical pulling motion.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground. Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Sit upright with your chest out and shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back. Pull the handles down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Adjust the seat so your knees fit snugly under the pads and place your feet flat on the floor.
Reach up and grab the handles with an underhand grip about shoulder-width apart.
Sit tall with your chest up and arms fully extended overhead.
Pull the handles down toward your upper chest by driving your elbows down and back.
Squeeze your upper back and lats at the bottom without leaning far backward.
Slowly let the handles rise until your arms are straight again while keeping control.
commonMistakes: Leaning far back and turning it into a row
Pulling the handles behind the neck
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears
Letting the weight slam back at the top
breathing: Inhale as the handles rise back up, and exhale as you pull them down toward your chest.

name: Reverse Grip Pull-Up
shortDescription: A vertical bodyweight pulling exercise with palms facing you, emphasizing the lats and biceps.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip. Pull your chin above the bar by flexing your elbows and squeezing your lats, then lower under control.
steps: Grab the bar with palms facing you (supinated grip), hands shoulder-width apart.
Hang with arms fully extended and scapula engaged.
Pull your chest up towards the bar, leading with your elbows.
Clear your chin above the bar, squeezing at the top.
Lower yourself back to the starting position with control.
breathing: Exhale pulling up, inhale lowering down.

name: Reverse-Grip Cable Lat Pulldown
shortDescription: A cable pulldown variation using an underhand (supinated) grip to emphasize the lower lats and biceps.
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown station and grasp the bar with a reverse (underhand) grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Pull the bar down to your upper chest, squeezing the shoulder blades together, then slowly return to starting position.
steps: Set the cable bar attachment and sit with knees secured under the pad.
Grasp the bar with a reverse (supinated) shoulder-width grip.
Lean back slightly, chest up, arms fully extended.
Pull the bar down to your upper chest by contracting your lats.
Slowly return the bar to the start position with control.
breathing: Exhale as you pull down, inhale as you return up.

name: Ring High Row
shortDescription: A suspended horizontal pulling movement using rings set high to target upper back and rear shoulder muscles.
instructions: Set up rings at an appropriate height and grip handles with palms facing in. Lean back with body rigid, arms extended, and feet forward. Pull your chest up to ring height, squeezing the shoulder blades together, then lower back under control.
steps: Adjust rings to chest height and grip handles with neutral grip.
Lean back with feet forward and body straight.
Engage core, keep arms extended.
Row your chest toward the rings, retracting shoulder blades.
Lower body back to start under control.
breathing: Exhale as you pull, inhale as you lower.

name: Rotational Push-Up
shortDescription: A push-up variation involving a torso rotation to challenge the chest, shoulders, triceps, and core.
instructions: From a push-up position, lower down as normal. As you press up, rotate your torso and extend one arm toward the ceiling, forming a T with your arms. Return to push-up position and repeat to the other side.
steps: Begin in a standard push-up position.
Lower your chest toward the floor.
As you press up, rotate your torso and lift one arm toward the ceiling.
Hold briefly, keeping hips square.
Return to start, repeat on the other side.
breathing: Exhale when pushing up and rotating, inhale when lowering.

name: Runner's Stretch
shortDescription: A flexibility exercise targeting the hip flexors and hamstrings by stepping one leg forward into a deep lunge.
instructions: Begin in a lunge position with one foot forward and the other leg extended back, keeping both hands on the floor. Sink your hips low toward the ground while keeping your chest lifted, feeling a stretch in the hip flexor of your rear leg and hamstring of your front leg.
steps: Step one foot forward into a lunge, keeping the rear leg extended.
Place both hands on either side of the front foot.
Lower your hips gently, keeping back leg straight.
Lift your chest for deeper stretch.
Hold, then switch legs.
breathing: Inhale as you get into position, exhale as you deepen the stretch.

name: Running
shortDescription: A repetitive aerobic movement that improves cardiovascular fitness and endurance.
instructions: Begin at a comfortable pace, maintaining upright posture and relaxed shoulders. Land lightly on your feet and use a natural arm swing for balance, adjusting speed and distance as fitness improves.
steps: Warm up with light walking or dynamic stretches.
Begin running at a moderate pace.
Maintain a steady breathing rhythm and upright posture.
Land lightly with a midfoot or forefoot strike.
Cool down with walking and stretching.
breathing: Exhale steadily as you push off, inhale as you recover.

name: Russian Twist on Stability Ball (Arms Straight)
aliases: Russian Twist (on stability ball arms straight)
shortDescription: An advanced core exercise targeting the obliques by rotating the torso while balanced on a stability ball with arms extended.
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with feet flat on the ground, then walk feet forward to roll down until your upper back is supported. Extend your arms straight in front and rotate your torso side to side, keeping arms aligned and abs engaged throughout.
steps: Sit on a stability ball and walk feet forward until shoulders rest on the ball.
Keep knees bent at 90 degrees, hips lifted in a bridge position.
Extend arms straight toward the ceiling with hands together.
Rotate torso and arms as one unit to the left.
Return to center, then rotate to the right, repeating for reps.
breathing: Exhale during each rotation, inhale as you return to center.

name: Scapula Dips
shortDescription: A bodyweight shoulder-blade dip on straight arms that trains scapular control and shoulder depression in a supported position.
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended in front of you. Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight. Lower your body by bending your elbows and retracting your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to squeeze a pencil between them. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to extend your elbows and return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Start in a straight-arm support on parallel bars or dip bars with your elbows locked and shoulders stacked over your hands.
Lift your chest and brace your trunk while keeping your legs still under you.
Lower your body a few inches by letting your shoulders rise toward your ears without bending your elbows.
Press down through your hands to pull your shoulders away from your ears and raise your body back up.
Repeat with slow, controlled shoulder movement while keeping your arms straight the whole time.
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows like a regular dip
Swinging the legs or torso to create momentum
Shrugging up and never pressing back down
Dropping too far and losing shoulder control
breathing: Inhale as you lower into the shoulder shrug, and exhale as you press down to lift your body back up.

name: Seated Alternating Dumbbell Curl
shortDescription: A seated exercise curling dumbbells alternately to build bicep size and strength.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Curl one dumbbell by flexing the elbow, then lower it and repeat with the opposite arm, keeping your torso stable.
steps: Sit with back against support and feet flat.
Hold dumbbells at your sides, palms facing in.
Curl one dumbbell while rotating palm up.
Lower to starting position and alternate arms.
Repeat, keeping back straight and elbows close to sides.
breathing: Exhale as you curl up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Seated Bent Over Back Stretch
shortDescription: A static stretch performed while seated, bending forward to lengthen the lower and upper back muscles.
instructions: Sit on the floor with legs extended or crossed. Hinge at the hips and slowly bend forward, reaching hands toward feet or out in front, keeping the back as relaxed as comfortable. Hold the stretch to gently lengthen the muscles along the spine.
steps: Sit comfortably on the floor, legs extended or crossed.
Hinge forward at the hips, keeping back relaxed.
Let arms reach forward toward feet or floor.
Lower your chest toward your knees, breathing deeply.
Hold the stretch and relax into the position.
breathing: Inhale before bending, exhale as you fold forward.

name: Seated Boat Stretch
shortDescription: A seated flexibility stretch focusing on the inner thighs and hips.
instructions: Sit on the floor and bring the soles of your feet together, letting knees fall out to the sides. Hold your feet or ankles and gently pull them in, leaning forward slightly to deepen the stretch through the groin and hips.
steps: Sit on the floor with back straight.
Bring soles of feet together and let knees drop out.
Hold ankles or feet for support.
Gently pull feet closer and lean forward slightly.
Hold position without bouncing.
formCues: Go only as far as comfortable
breathing: Breathe deeply and regularly while holding the stretch.

name: Seated Cable Row (V-Grip)
aliases: cable reverse-grip straight back seated high row
shortDescription: A compound cable exercise targeting the mid-back and lats using a V-grip handle.
instructions: Sit on a cable row station with feet on the platform and grasp the V-grip handle. Keep your back straight and pull the handle towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and bending your elbows. Control the return to the starting position.
steps: Sit on the cable row machine and place feet on the foot platform.
Grab the V-grip handle with both hands, arms extended.
Maintain a straight back and slight knee bend.
Pull the handle toward your abdomen, retracting your shoulder blades.
Slowly extend arms to return to the starting position.
breathing: Exhale as you row back, inhale as you extend forward.

name: Seated Cable Row (Wide-Grip)
shortDescription: A cable row variation using a wide grip to target the upper back and rear deltoids.
instructions: Sit on the cable row machine with feet on the platform and grab the wide grip handle. Pull the bar towards the upper abdomen, retracting the shoulder blades, and return to the starting position with control.
steps: Sit down and place feet against the platform.
Grasp the wide grip attachment with both hands.
Keep your torso upright, arms fully extended.
Pull the handle toward your upper abdomen, squeezing your upper back.
Slowly release back to the start position.
breathing: Exhale as you pull, inhale as you extend arms.

name: Seated Calf Stretch
shortDescription: A stretch performed while seated, focusing on lengthening the calf muscles.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended. Loop a towel or band around the ball of one foot, keeping your knee straight, and gently pull your toes toward you, feeling a stretch in the calf. Hold and repeat on the other side.
steps: Sit with legs extended straight in front.
Loop a towel or band around one foot's ball.
Keep knee straight and pull your toes toward you.
Hold position, feeling stretch in calf.
Repeat on the other leg.
breathing: Breathe steadily and deeply throughout the stretch.

name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
shortDescription: A pressing movement targeting the shoulders, performed seated with dumbbells.
instructions: Sit upright with back supported and hold dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Press the weights up overhead until arms are extended, then lower back down under control.
steps: Sit on a bench with back support and feet flat.
Hold dumbbells at shoulder height, elbows bent.
Press dumbbells overhead until arms are extended.
Pause briefly at the top.
Lower dumbbells to starting position.
breathing: Exhale as you press up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Seated Lower Back Stretch
shortDescription: A gentle forward reaching stretch to relieve tension in the lower back while seated.
instructions: Sit on the floor or chair with your legs extended or crossed. Reach both arms forward and slowly bend at the hips, lowering your torso and arms toward your feet, feeling the stretch across your lower back. Hold the position, keeping the back relaxed.
steps: Sit with legs extended or crossed.
Reach both arms straight in front of you.
Slowly bend at your hips to lower your torso forward.
Relax your back and shoulders.
Hold stretch, then return to upright position.
breathing: Inhale deeply before bending, exhale as you reach forward.

name: Seated Shoulder Flexor, Depressor, Retractor Stretch
aliases: Stretching - Seated Shoulder Flexor Depresor Retractor
roller seated shoulder flexor depressor retractor
shortDescription: A seated stretch focusing on improving flexibility and mobility in the front, lower, and rear shoulder muscles.
instructions: Sit upright on the floor or a chair with feet flat. Reach one or both arms overhead, then gently pull the shoulders downward and backward to open the chest and lengthen the front and rear deltoids. Maintain a tall posture and breathe deeply.
steps: Sit tall with feet flat.
Lift arms overhead, reaching upward.
Gently depress the shoulders away from your ears.
Retract shoulder blades by pulling them slightly together.
Hold for a stretch, breathing evenly.
breathing: Inhale to begin, exhale as you stretch deeper.

name: Seated Straight Arm Twist
shortDescription: Seated torso rotation with arms straight to target the obliques and core stability.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet elevated or flat. Extend both arms straight out in front of you, clasping your hands together. Rotate your torso to one side, keeping arms straight, then rotate to the other side while engaging your core throughout.
steps: Sit with knees bent, feet either flat or off the ground.
Extend arms straight forward, clasping hands.
Lean back slightly with a neutral spine.
Rotate torso to one side, arms following movement.
Return to center and repeat to opposite side.
commonMistakes: Using just arms instead of rotating the torso
Moving too quickly or without control
breathing: Exhale as you twist, inhale returning to center.

name: Seated Twist on Stability Ball
shortDescription: A rotational core exercise performed while seated on a stability ball to target the obliques.
instructions: Sit upright on a stability ball, feet flat and hip-width apart. Twist your torso to one side while keeping hips steady, then return to center and repeat on the other side.
steps: Sit on a stability ball with feet flat, core engaged.
Hold arms in front or across chest.
Rotate your torso to the right, keeping hips stationary.
Pause, then twist to the left.
Return to center, repeat alternately.
breathing: Exhale during the twist, inhale as you return to center.

name: Seated Twist Stretch (Straight Arm)
shortDescription: A flexibility exercise involving seated trunk rotation to stretch the obliques and lower back.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and back straight. Extend your right arm behind you and twist your torso to the right, keeping your left arm straight across your knee for leverage, hold, and repeat on the other side.
steps: Sit upright with legs extended forward.
Cross your left leg over your right if desired for more stretch.
Extend your right arm straight behind you for support.
Twist your torso toward your right, using the opposite arm for leverage.
Hold, then repeat to the other side.
breathing: Breathe steadily while holding the stretch.

name: Seated Wide Angle Pose Sequence
aliases: Stretching - Seated Wide Angle Pose Sequence
shortDescription: A sequence of stretches performed in a wide-legged seated position to improve hip, hamstring, and lower back flexibility.
instructions: Sit upright and spread your legs as wide as is comfortable. With a straight back, hinge forward at the hips and walk your hands out in front or toward each leg, deepening the stretch in the inner thighs and lower back. Carefully progress through variations to target various muscle groups.
steps: Sit with legs extended wide apart.
Keep knees and toes pointing upward.
Sit tall and hinge forward at hips.
Walk hands forward, maintaining flat back.
Hold stretch or gently move side to side.
breathing: Inhale to prepare, exhale as you stretch forward.

name: Shoulder-Width Pull-Up
shortDescription: A classic bodyweight exercise targeting the lats and upper back with a shoulder-width overhand grip.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing away. Initiate movement from the back and pull your chin over the bar, then lower yourself in a controlled manner back to the starting position.
steps: Grip bar shoulder-width, palms away.
Hang with arms fully extended.
Pull up until chin clears the bar.
Pause briefly at the top.
Lower down slowly to full extension.
breathing: Exhale pulling up, inhale lowering down.

name: Side Bend on Stability Ball
shortDescription: A lateral flexion movement performed on a stability ball to target the obliques.
instructions: Lie sideways over a stability ball, feet anchored, and hands behind head. Crunch your torso upward in a side-bending motion, then lower with control.
steps: Position yourself sideways on a stability ball, feet braced.
Place top hand behind head, other hand for balance.
Lower your torso over the ball for a stretch.
Contract your side to lift torso up in a side bend.
Lower back down with control, repeat.
breathing: Exhale as you crunch up, inhale as you lower.

name: Side Bend Stretch
shortDescription: A standing lateral bending stretch focusing on lengthening the obliques and side body muscles.
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart or sit upright. Raise one arm overhead and lean to the opposite side, feeling a stretch along your side and back muscles. Hold briefly, then return to start and repeat on the other side.
steps: Stand or sit tall with spine neutral.
Raise right arm overhead.
Slowly bend torso to the left, keeping hips steady.
Hold stretch for time, feeling the pull in your side.
Return to start and repeat on the other side.
breathing: Exhale as you bend, inhale as you return to upright.

name: Side Plank
shortDescription: A core-strengthening hold performed on one side, engaging the obliques and stabilizing hip muscles.
instructions: Lie on one side with your elbow under your shoulder and legs extended. Lift your hips off the ground to form a straight line from head to feet, bracing your abs and holding the position while breathing steadily.
steps: Lie on your side, elbow stacked under shoulder.
Stack your feet or place one in front of the other.
Raise hips up to create a straight line.
Hold body in line, keeping abs and glutes engaged.
Lower hips gently to finish; switch sides.
breathing: Breathe steadily throughout the hold.

name: Side Wrist Pull Stretch
shortDescription: A standing or seated stretch for the wrist and forearm, involving lateral arm extension and gentle pulling.
instructions: Stand or sit upright, extend one arm overhead or to the side, and grasp the wrist with your opposite hand. Gently pull the wrist to the side to stretch the forearm and wrist muscles, avoiding excessive force.
steps: Stand or sit tall.
Extend one arm overhead or outward.
Hold your wrist with the opposite hand.
Pull gently toward one side.
Hold, then switch sides.
breathing: Exhale as you stretch, inhale to reset or switch sides.

name: Single Dumbbell Stiff-Leg Deadlift
shortDescription: A stiff-legged deadlift variation using a single dumbbell to target the hamstrings and lower back.
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell with both hands in front of your thighs. With a slight bend at the knees, hinge at the hips to lower the dumbbell until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings and then return to the starting position.
steps: Stand upright holding a dumbbell with both hands at your thighs.
Keep a slight bend in your knees and your back flat.
Hinge forward at the hips, lowering the dumbbell towards the floor.
Pause at the bottom while feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Return to the upright position by driving hips forward.
breathing: Inhale lowering, exhale as you stand back up.

name: Single Leg Stretch (Bent Knee)
shortDescription: A floor stretch to target hip flexors and hamstrings by pulling one bent leg towards the chest.
instructions: Lie on your back with both knees bent. Grip one shin and gently pull that knee towards your chest, feeling the stretch in your hip and glutes. Maintain a neutral spine and hold before switching legs.
steps: Lie on your back with both legs bent.
Grasp one knee with both hands.
Pull the knee toward your chest gently.
Hold the stretch, keeping your back flat.
Switch sides and repeat.
breathing: Exhale as you pull the knee in, inhale as you release.

name: Single Straight Leg Stretch
shortDescription: A floor stretch focusing on the hamstrings by raising and holding one straight leg toward the body.
instructions: Lie on your back, legs extended. Lift one leg straight up, keeping it as straight as possible, and hold the calf or thigh to gently pull it closer, feeling a stretch in the hamstrings and calves. Then switch legs.
steps: Lie on your back with both legs straight.
Lift one leg up while keeping it straight.
Hold the back of your thigh or calf gently.
Pull the leg slightly toward your chest.
Hold and switch legs.
formCues: Point or flex foot for deeper stretch
breathing: Exhale as you stretch the leg, inhale as you lower it.

name: Sit-Up
shortDescription: A classic ab exercise involving lifting the torso from the floor to a seated position to build core strength.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the ground. Place your hands behind your head or across your chest, engage your core, and lift your torso all the way up to your knees, then lower back down with control.
steps: Lie flat on your back with knees bent and feet anchored.
Place hands behind your head or across your chest.
Engage your core to lift your torso to a seated position.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
breathing: Exhale as you rise, inhale as you lower.

name: Sit-Up
shortDescription: A classic abdominal exercise that lifts the torso upward from a lying position.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Cross your arms over your chest or place hands behind your head, and use your abdominals to lift your upper body toward your knees, then lower back down with control.
steps: Lie down with knees bent and feet flat.
Cross arms over chest or place hands behind head.
Engage your core and raise your torso towards your knees.
Pause briefly at the top.
Slowly lower back to starting position.
breathing: Exhale on the ascent, inhale on the descent.

name: Sled 45 Degree Calf Press
shortDescription: A seated calf exercise on a 45-degree sled where you press through the balls of your feet to raise and lower the heels.
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a 45-degree angle. Place your feet on the sled platform with your toes pointing forward. Push the sled platform away from you by extending your ankles and calves. Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the sled platform back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Sit in the 45-degree sled machine and place the balls of both feet on the lower edge of the platform with your heels hanging off.
Straighten your legs enough to support the sled and keep a small bend in your knees.
Let your heels drop down by bending at the ankles until you feel a stretch in your calves.
Press through the balls of your feet and raise your heels as high as you can.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping your legs steady.
Lower your heels under control back to the stretched position and repeat.
formCues: Drive through the balls of feet
Lift heels as high as possible
commonMistakes: Bending and straightening the knees instead of moving only at the ankles
Placing the whole foot flat on the platform so the heels cannot drop
Bouncing out of the bottom instead of lowering under control
Turning the toes excessively in or out during the rep
breathing: Inhale as you lower your heels, and exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise them.

name: Sled 45 Degree Wide Leg Press
shortDescription: A compound lower body movement targeting the quadriceps with emphasis on glutes and adductors due to a wide foot stance.
instructions: Sit on a 45-degree leg press machine with feet placed wider than hip-width on the platform. Push the platform up to release the safety handles and slowly lower it toward your chest, then drive through your heels to press the weight back up.
steps: Adjust the seat and load desired weight.
Sit with your back and head against the pad, feet flat and wide on the platform.
Release the safety handles and grasp the side handles.
Bend your knees to lower the platform until your thighs reach 90 degrees or just past.
Press through your heels to extend your legs fully, but avoid locking out the knees.
formCues: Keep knees in line with toes
Do not lock knees at top
breathing: Inhale as you lower the platform, exhale as you press it back up.

name: Sled Hack Squat
shortDescription: A machine squat on an angled sled with your back supported to train the quads, glutes, and legs through a controlled range.
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable position for your height. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly pointed outwards. Hold onto the handles or bars for stability. Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight and chest up. Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below. Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to raise your body back up to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Stand on the sled platform with your back against the pad and your shoulders under the pads.
Place your feet about shoulder-width apart on the platform with toes turned slightly out, then grip the handles.
Unlock the sled and lower yourself by bending your knees and hips until your thighs reach parallel or your deepest controlled position.
Keep your full foot planted against the platform as your knees track in line with your toes.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips and return the sled upward.
Stop just short of locking your knees, then continue into the next repetition.
commonMistakes: Feet placed too low so the heels lift off the platform
Knees collapsing inward during the lowering phase
Lower back rounding or hips peeling off the pad
Bouncing out of the bottom instead of pausing briefly
breathing: Inhale as you lower the sled, then exhale as you press it back up.

name: Smith Chair Squat
shortDescription: A Smith machine squat variation that targets the quads while training the glutes and hamstrings through a controlled bar path.
instructions: Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to a comfortable position. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Place the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps. Engage your core and keep your chest up as you slowly lower your body by bending your knees and hips. Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go. Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Set the Smith bar across your upper back and stand with your feet about shoulder-width apart, slightly in front of the bar path.
Unrack the bar and brace your core with your chest up and eyes forward.
Bend your knees and hips together to sit straight down under control.
Lower until your thighs are about parallel to the floor or slightly below while keeping your heels down.
Drive through your midfoot and heels to stand back up until your knees and hips are fully extended.
Lock out tall, then repeat for the next rep.
commonMistakes: Feet set directly under the bar so the knees travel too far forward.
Heels lift off the floor at the bottom.
Knees cave inward during the ascent.
Lower back rounds as the hips drop.
breathing: Inhale and brace before you lower, then exhale as you drive back up to standing.

name: Smith Machine Calf Raise
aliases: smith one leg floor calf raise
shortDescription: A standing calf raise using a Smith machine to isolate and strengthen the calf muscles.
instructions: Stand upright under the Smith machine bar, with the bar resting across your upper traps and feet shoulder-width apart. Raise your heels to stand on your toes, contract your calves, and slowly lower back to the ground.
steps: Set up the Smith machine bar at shoulder height.
Step under the bar, position it across your upper traps, and unrack.
Stand upright with feet hip to shoulder-width apart.
Rise onto the balls of your feet by extending your ankles.
Pause at the top, then lower your heels in a controlled manner.
breathing: Exhale as you rise, inhale as you lower.

name: Smith Machine Deadlift
shortDescription: A guided barbell deadlift performed with a Smith machine to build lower back, glutes, and hamstrings strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart beneath the Smith bar. Hold the bar with an overhand grip, hinge at your hips, bend knees, and keep your back neutral. Extend hips and knees to lift the bar along the rails, then return slowly under control.
steps: Position feet shoulder-width beneath the Smith bar.
Grip the bar just outside your knees with both hands.
Hinge hips back and lower torso, keeping chest up.
Extend hips and knees to lift the bar along the guides.
Lower the bar with control to the starting position.
commonMistakes: Lifting with arms instead of legs
Letting the bar drift away from body
breathing: Inhale as you lower the bar, exhale as you lift.

name: Smith Machine Leg Press
shortDescription: A machine-based variation of leg press performed with a Smith machine barbell in a supine or incline position.
instructions: Lie on the floor or bench under a Smith machine with feet placed on the bar wider than shoulder-width. Unlock the bar and press upwards with your legs, lowering until your knees reach a 90-degree angle before pressing back to the start.
steps: Set the safety stops and load the appropriate weight.
Lie under the Smith bar and position feet wider than shoulder-width on the bar.
Unrack the bar by rotating your feet upward.
Lower the bar slowly toward your torso until legs form roughly a 90-degree angle.
Press the bar up by extending your knees and hips.
breathing: Inhale as you lower the bar, exhale as you press upward.

name: Smith Seated Shoulder Press
shortDescription: A seated overhead press on a Smith machine that trains the delts and triceps with a fixed bar path.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that the handles are at shoulder level. Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the floor. Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and lift them off the supports, extending your arms fully. Lower the handles down to shoulder level, keeping your elbows slightly bent. Press the handles up overhead until your arms are fully extended. Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to shoulder level. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Set a bench or seat under the Smith bar so the bar starts around shoulder height when you sit down.
Sit tall with your back supported, plant your feet flat, and grip the bar just wider than shoulder width with palms forward.
Unrack the bar and hold it above your upper chest with wrists stacked over elbows.
Lower the bar in a straight path until it reaches about chin to shoulder level.
Press the bar straight up until your arms are extended overhead.
Lower the bar with control to the start position and repeat.
Finish by locking out the top and rotating the bar to re-rack it on the hooks.
commonMistakes: Lowering the bar behind the head
Flaring the elbows straight out to the sides
Arching the lower back off the bench
Letting the wrists bend back excessively
breathing: Inhale as you lower the bar to shoulder level, and exhale as you press it overhead.

name: Spell Caster
shortDescription: A standing dumbbell core move where you hinge and rotate side to side, reaching the weight toward each foot to train the obliques.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand. Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the waist, keeping your back straight. Extend your arm with the dumbbell towards the opposite foot, rotating your torso as you do so. Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Stand tall with your feet about shoulder-width apart, holding one dumbbell with both hands in front of your hips.
Soften your knees and hinge slightly at your hips while keeping your chest up and back flat.
Rotate your torso and lower the dumbbell toward the outside of one foot.
Drive through your hips and return to the center with the dumbbell back in front of your hips.
Rotate to the other side and lower the dumbbell toward the outside of the opposite foot.
Continue alternating sides with controlled movement.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back as the dumbbell lowers
Bending mostly through the spine instead of hinging at the hips
Letting the knees cave inward during the reach
Swinging the dumbbell quickly instead of controlling each side
breathing: Inhale as you lower and rotate toward one foot, then exhale as you return to center and switch sides.

name: Spine Stretch
shortDescription: A floor stretch focusing on lengthening the spine and improving flexibility in the lower back and hamstrings.
instructions: Sit with legs extended and feet flexed, arms extended in front of you. Exhale as you reach forward, articulating the spine and stretching through your fingertips. Hold the stretch, then slowly return to start.
steps: Sit on floor with legs extended and feet flexed.
Extend arms straight ahead at shoulder height.
Inhale, then as you exhale, reach forward, articulating your spine.
Stretch forward as far as comfortable, keeping legs straight.
Hold and return slowly to start.
breathing: Exhale as you stretch forward, inhale as you return to start.

name: Squat
shortDescription: A foundational lower body movement that strengthens the quads, glutes, and hamstrings by bending and straightening the knees and hips.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out. Lower your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair, keeping your chest upright, then drive through your heels to stand back up.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly outward.
Brace core and keep chest up.
Initiate movement by pushing hips back and bending knees.
Descend until thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Push through heels to return to standing.
breathing: Inhale as you lower down, exhale as you stand up.

name: Stability Ball Leg Curl
aliases: dumbbell one arm concentration curl (on stability ball)
shortDescription: A hamstring-focused curl using a stability ball for both resistance and core engagement.
instructions: Lie on your back with your heels on a stability ball. Lift your hips, then curl the ball toward your glutes by flexing your knees, and extend legs back out while keeping hips elevated.
steps: Lie face up on the floor, arms at your sides, heels on a stability ball.
Lift hips to form a straight line from shoulders to heels.
Bend knees to roll the ball toward your glutes.
Pause and squeeze your hamstrings.
Extend your legs to roll the ball back out.
breathing: Exhale as you curl in, inhale as you extend out.

name: Stability Ball Leg Elevated Crunch
shortDescription: A crunch variation where the legs rest on a stability ball, increasing core engagement and instability.
instructions: Lie on your back with legs bent and calves resting on a stability ball, hands behind your head. Engage your core and lift your shoulders toward your knees, keeping your lower back on the floor, then return to the starting position.
steps: Place stability ball in front of you and sit down.
Lie back and position your calves and feet on top of the ball with knees bent.
Place your hands lightly behind your head or crossed on your chest.
Engage your core and curl your upper body toward your knees.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower back down without losing control.
formCues: Keep lower back pressed to floor
Do not pull with your neck
breathing: Exhale as you crunch up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Stairs Calf Stretch
shortDescription: A stretch performed using stairs to lengthen and increase flexibility in the calf muscles.
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step with your heels hanging off. Slowly lower your heels toward the ground, feeling the stretch in your calves. Hold, then return to starting position or alternate legs.
steps: Stand with the balls of your feet on a step or stair.
Support yourself with a railing, wall, or sturdy object.
Lower your heels slowly below the step to feel a stretch in your calves.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Raise heels back up and repeat as desired.
formCues: Keep knees straight but not locked
breathing: Exhale as you lower into the stretch, inhale as you return.

name: Standing Adductor Stretch
shortDescription: A static stretch targeting the inner thighs by lunging to the side and holding.
instructions: Stand with feet wide apart and slowly bend one knee while keeping the other leg straight. Lean into the bent knee side, feeling the stretch along your inner thigh. Hold, then switch sides.
steps: Stand wide with feet apart.
Shift weight to one leg, bending at the knee.
Keep the opposite leg straight and toes forward.
Lean gently toward the bent knee for a deep inner thigh stretch.
Hold, then switch to the other side.
breathing: Breathe deeply and steadily while holding the stretch.

name: Standing Back Rotation Stretch
shortDescription: A standing twisting stretch targeting the obliques and rotational muscles of the back.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and arms out in front or at your sides. Slowly rotate your upper body to one side, keeping hips square, to feel a gentle stretch along your back and waist. Hold, return to center, and repeat to the other side.
steps: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend arms at shoulder height or place hands on hips.
Rotate torso gently to one side without moving hips.
Hold stretch for desired time.
Return to center and repeat on other side.
breathing: Exhale as you twist, inhale as you return to center.

name: Standing Bench Calf Stretch
shortDescription: A static stretch using a bench to lengthen the calf muscles.
instructions: Stand facing a bench and place your toes up onto the edge with your heel on the ground. Lean forward slightly until you feel a stretch through your calf. Hold for the desired time and repeat with the other leg.
steps: Stand in front of a bench or sturdy surface.
Place the ball of one foot onto the edge, keeping heel on the floor.
Lean your body forward while keeping your knee straight.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Switch legs and repeat.
formCues: Feel stretch in calf, not ankle
commonMistakes: Letting the foot roll inward or outward
breathing: Exhale as you relax into the stretch, inhale as you ease off.

name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
shortDescription: A static stretch targeting the hamstring muscles along the back of the upper leg.
instructions: Stand tall and keep knees slightly bent. Hinge forward at the hips, lowering your torso while reaching towards your toes to feel a gentle stretch in your hamstrings. Hold the stretch without bouncing.
steps: Stand up straight with feet hip-width apart.
Keep knees soft, and hinge forward at the hips.
Lower your torso toward your legs while reaching for your toes.
Stop when you feel a gentle stretch in your hamstrings.
Hold for the desired duration, then return to standing.
breathing: Exhale as you hinge forward, inhale as you return upright.

name: Standing Hip Flexor and Abdominal Stretch
shortDescription: A standing stretch focusing on opening the hips and lengthening the abdominal muscles by reaching overhead and leaning back.
instructions: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart. Raise both arms overhead and gently arch your back, lifting your chest and looking up at the ceiling. Engage your glutes and extend hips forward to feel a stretch through the front of your hips and abdomen.
steps: Stand upright with feet shoulder-width apart.
Lift arms overhead, palms facing forward.
Arch back gently, lifting chest and looking upward.
Shift hips forward slightly to increase hip flexor stretch.
Hold and breathe for desired duration.
breathing: Inhale before arching; exhale as you hold the stretch.

name: Standing Knee Raise Stretch
shortDescription: A stretch that targets the hip flexors and glutes by pulling one knee toward the chest while standing.
instructions: Stand upright and balance on one foot. Lift one knee up toward your chest, grasping it with both hands and pulling gently until you feel a stretch in the hip and glute. Hold briefly, lower, and repeat on the other leg.
steps: Stand tall with feet together.
Lift one knee toward your chest.
Grasp your knee with both hands.
Pull the knee gently toward your chest, feeling the stretch.
Hold, then lower and repeat on the opposite leg.
formCues: Use arms to pull knee gently
breathing: Exhale as you pull the knee up, inhale as you release.

name: Standing Lateral Stretch
shortDescription: A standing side-bending stretch to target the obliques and lengthen the muscles along the side of the torso.
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart; raise one arm overhead and gently lean to the opposite side. Feel a stretch along the side of your torso and hold, then switch sides.
steps: Stand upright, feet hip-width apart.
Raise one arm overhead, keeping it straight.
Place other hand on hip or let it hang at side.
Gently lean to the opposite side, keeping hips square.
Hold, then return to center and repeat on the other side.
breathing: Inhale to lengthen, exhale as you lean into the stretch.

name: Standing Quadriceps Stretch
shortDescription: A standing stretch that targets the quadriceps for improved flexibility.
instructions: While standing, bend one knee and reach back to grasp your foot or ankle. Pull your heel towards your glutes while keeping your knees close together and torso upright. Use support if needed for balance.
steps: Stand upright, balancing on one foot.
Bend the opposite knee, reaching back for the foot.
Pull heel gently towards glute while keeping knees together.
Keep torso tall and hips level.
Hold the stretch, then switch legs.
breathing: Exhale as you pull heel in, inhale as you release.

name: Standing Reach Up Back Rotation Stretch
shortDescription: A dynamic stretch involving reaching overhead and rotating to open up the chest and upper back.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Reach both arms overhead and gently arch your upper back while rotating your torso to one side, focusing on stretching through your chest and shoulders. Hold for a moment, return to center, and repeat to the other side.
steps: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart.
Raise both arms overhead.
Gently arch your upper back and chest.
Rotate your torso to one side while maintaining the stretch.
Return to center, then rotate to the other side.
breathing: Inhale before reaching up; exhale as you rotate and stretch.

name: Standing Side Bend Stretch (Bent Arm)
shortDescription: A side bending stretch performed while standing to lengthen the obliques and lats.
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, raise one arm overhead and bend it, then lean to the side while keeping the torso in one plane, hold the stretch, and repeat on the other side.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Raise one arm overhead, bending the elbow comfortably.
Lean sideways away from the raised arm to stretch the side of your torso.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Switch sides and repeat.
breathing: Breathe deeply throughout the stretch.

name: Standing Wheel Rollout
shortDescription: An advanced rollout exercise that stretches and strengthens the abdominals, chest, shoulders, and back.
instructions: Begin standing with an ab wheel on the floor in front of you. Holding the handles, slowly roll the wheel forward as you lower your torso, keeping your arms straight, until your body is almost parallel to the ground. Use your core and lats to pull yourself back up.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ab wheel.
Bend forward and place the wheel on the floor in front of your feet.
Roll the wheel forward, extending your arms and torso while keeping your core tight.
Lower your body until you're just above the floor, maintaining a straight line from head to heels.
Reverse the motion by pulling the wheel back toward your feet, returning to the start.
breathing: Inhale as you roll out, exhale as you pull back in.

name: Stationary Bike
shortDescription: A cardio exercise performed while seated, pedaling a stationary machine to boost heart rate and endurance.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and handlebar position for comfort and knee alignment. Pedal continuously at your desired intensity, maintaining good posture throughout the workout.
steps: Set seat and handlebar to appropriate heights.
Sit upright and grip the handlebars lightly.
Place feet securely on the pedals.
Begin pedaling at a comfortable resistance and pace.
Continue for your intended duration, maintaining good form.
breathing: Breathe evenly and deeply throughout the session.

name: Straight-Leg Up Crunch
shortDescription: A crunch variant with straight legs held vertically to challenge upper abdominals.
instructions: Lie on your back with legs straight up in the air, arms at your sides or behind your head. Engage your core to lift your head, neck, and shoulders toward your feet, then lower back down with control.
steps: Lie on back, legs straight up, feet flexed.
Place arms beside you or hands behind head.
Engage core, lift head and shoulders toward legs.
Pause at top, exhale.
Lower down with control.
breathing: Exhale as you crunch up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Supine Dumbbell Biceps Curl
shortDescription: A biceps isolation curl performed lying face up for strict form and minimal cheating.
instructions: Lie supine (face up) on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended. Keeping your upper arms still, curl the weights towards your shoulders, then lower under control.
steps: Lie flat on your back holding dumbbells at your sides, arms fully extended.
Keep palms facing forward (supinated grip).
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, elbows stationary.
Squeeze your biceps at the top.
Lower the weights slowly to the start position.
breathing: Exhale as you curl up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Treadmill Running
shortDescription: A popular form of indoor cardiovascular exercise performed at varying intensities on a treadmill machine.
instructions: Set your desired speed and incline on the treadmill. Start jogging or running, ensuring good posture with a natural stride. Adjust settings as needed for intensity and duration.
steps: Step onto the treadmill and position feet on the sides.
Select preferred speed and incline.
Begin running, holding onto the handrails if necessary for balance.
Focus on smooth strides and upright posture.
Cool down by gradually reducing speed before stopping.
breathing: Inhale through nose, exhale through mouth in rhythm with strides.

name: Triceps Dips
shortDescription: A bodyweight exercise that targets the triceps through a pressing motion while lowering and raising the body.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with hands next to your hips and legs extended forward. Slide your hips off the seat and lower your body by bending your elbows to about 90 degrees, then push yourself back up by straightening your arms.
steps: Sit on a bench or chair with hands gripping the edge.
Extend legs forward and slide hips off the seat.
Lower body by bending elbows to 90 degrees.
Press through palms to straighten arms and lift body.
Repeat for reps.
formCues: Keep elbows close to your body
breathing: Inhale as you lower down, exhale as you push up.

name: Twisting Crunch
shortDescription: A crunch variation with a rotation to engage the obliques and upper abdominals.
instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent, hands behind head. Crunch up while rotating one elbow toward the opposite knee, then return and alternate sides.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat.
Place hands lightly behind your head.
Crunch up, twisting your torso to bring one shoulder toward the opposite knee.
Return to start and repeat to the other side.
Alternate sides for each repetition.
breathing: Exhale as you crunch and twist, inhale as you return.

name: V-Up
shortDescription: A dynamic core exercise where both legs and torso are lifted to meet in a 'V' position, activating the abdominals.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with arms extended overhead and legs straight. Simultaneously raise your legs and upper body, reaching your hands towards your feet to form a 'V' shape, then lower back down in control.
steps: Lie on your back with arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Engage your core to lift your legs and torso simultaneously.
Reach your hands toward your feet at the top, forming a 'V' shape.
Pause briefly, squeezing your abs.
Lower down slowly to starting position.
breathing: Exhale when lifting up, inhale as you lower down.

name: Vertical Leg Raise (on parallel bars)
aliases: straight leg raise on dip bars
hanging leg raise on parallel bars
shortDescription: A bodyweight core exercise on parallel bars where you raise straight legs in front of you under control.
instructions: Hang from the parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body straight. Engage your core and lift your legs up in front of you, keeping them straight. Continue lifting until your legs are parallel to the ground or slightly higher. Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Support yourself on the parallel bars with straight arms and your legs hanging together.
Pull your shoulders down and brace your midsection to stop your body from swinging.
Keep your knees straight and lift your legs forward in front of you.
Raise your legs until they reach hip height or slightly higher.
Pause briefly at the top without leaning back.
Lower your legs with control to the start and reset before the next repetition.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso to start each rep
Bending the knees as the legs rise
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears
Dropping the legs quickly on the way down
breathing: Exhale as you lift your legs, then inhale as you lower them back to the start.

name: Walk Wave Machine
shortDescription: A low-impact cardio machine utilizing a unique lateral gliding motion to engage lower body muscles and improve endurance.
instructions: Step onto the wave machine and grip the handles if available. Begin with a slow pace, moving feet in a side-to-side, skating motion. Gradually increase resistance and speed as you feel comfortable.
steps: Step onto the machine and position feet on pedals.
Grip handles for stability.
Initiate movement by pushing one leg outward while the other follows.
Maintain a rhythmic, lateral gliding motion.
Continue for the desired time or intensity.
breathing: Keep breath steady and controlled throughout.

name: Weighted Leg Extension Crunch
shortDescription: A core exercise combining a crunch with a resisted leg extension to engage the abdominals.
instructions: Lie on your back holding a weight at your chest, legs lifted and knees bent. Extend your legs out as you crunch upward, then return to the start position.
steps: Lie flat on your back with knees bent, holding a weight across your chest.
Lift your legs to tabletop position (knees over hips, 90-degree bend).
Simultaneously extend your legs outward and crunch your upper body up.
Pause briefly at peak contraction.
Return arms and legs to the starting position.
breathing: Exhale as you crunch and extend, inhale as you return.

name: Weighted Lying Twist
shortDescription: A core rotational movement that targets the obliques using added resistance.
instructions: Lie flat on your back, arms extended to your sides and holding a weight between your feet or over chest. Rotate your lower body side-to-side while stabilizing your upper body.
steps: Lie on your back with arms outstretched for support.
Hold a weight between your feet (or over chest).
Lift your legs up to a 90-degree angle.
Slowly rotate your legs to one side, keeping shoulders down.
Return to center and repeat on the other side.
breathing: Exhale as you twist, inhale as you return to center.

name: Weighted Sissy Squat
shortDescription: A quad-focused squat variation done on the balls of the feet with a backward torso lean while holding added weight.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing slightly outward. Hold a weight in front of your chest with both hands, or place a barbell across your upper back. Keeping your chest up and your core engaged, slowly lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips. Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or as low as you can comfortably go. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Stand tall with your feet about hip-width apart and hold the weight securely in front of your chest or across your upper back.
Rise onto the balls of your feet and brace your midsection.
Lean your torso back in one straight line from knees to shoulders as your knees travel forward.
Lower under control by bending your knees until you feel a strong stretch through the front of your thighs.
Drive through the balls of your feet and straighten your knees to return to the start.
Finish tall and reset your balance before the next repetition.
commonMistakes: Bending forward at the hips during the descent
Dropping the heels and shifting weight backward
Lowering too fast and bouncing out of the bottom
breathing: Inhale as you lower under control, then exhale as you drive back up to the starting position.

name: Weighted Standing Curl
shortDescription: A standing biceps exercise where you curl weights from your sides up toward your shoulders with palms facing forward.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward. Keep your elbows close to your torso and exhale as you curl the weights up to shoulder level. Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the weights back down to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
steps: Stand tall with a weight in each hand at your sides and turn your palms forward.
Set your feet about hip-width apart and keep your elbows pinned near your ribs.
Curl the weights upward by bending your elbows until your hands reach shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top without letting your elbows drift forward.
Lower the weights back down with control until your arms are fully straightened at your sides.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso to lift the weights
Letting the elbows drift forward at the top
Dropping the weights quickly on the way down
Bending the wrists back instead of keeping them neutral
breathing: Exhale as you curl the weights up, and inhale as you lower them back down under control.

name: Wide Grip Cable Lat Pulldown
shortDescription: A cable exercise performed with a wide grip that primarily targets the latissimus dorsi to build back width and upper body strength.
instructions: Sit at the cable pulldown machine and grip the wide bar with hands wider than shoulder width and palms facing forward. Engage your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back, and pull the bar down to your upper chest while keeping your back straight. Slowly return the bar to the starting position while controlling the resistance.
steps: Adjust the thigh pad and sit at the cable pulldown machine.
Grip the wide bar overhead with a pronated (overhand) grip, hands wider than shoulders.
Lean back slightly, brace your core, and retract your shoulder blades.
Pull the bar down to your upper chest, driving elbows down and out.
Pause briefly, then slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
breathing: Exhale as you pull the bar down, inhale as you return to start.

name: Wrist Circles
shortDescription: A warm-up and mobility exercise to increase wrist flexibility and circulation in the forearms.
instructions: Extend your arms in front or to your sides. Make fists or keep hands open, then slowly rotate wrists in circular motions, both clockwise and counterclockwise.
steps: Extend arms out in front or to sides.
Make fists or keep hands open.
Slowly rotate wrists in circular motions.
Perform several circles in one direction.
Switch direction and repeat.
breathing: Breathe evenly throughout the movement.
